Title: Arboviruses manipulate rice’s volatile emissions, protecting insect vectors from natural enemies in the field

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Nipponbare; transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirect defense signal attracting parasitoids and repelling vectors
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Volatile emission from rice aerial tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces MeSA-mediated defense by targeting OsMYC2, lowering OsBSMT1 expression and parasitoid attraction while increasing vector preference
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; coimmunoprecipitation; in vitro pull-down; BiFC; transgenic overexpression in rice; fractionation; behavioral assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights viral effector target for engineering resistance to preserve OsMYC2 nuclear activity and MeSA defense
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RSV NS2 specifically interacts with OsMYC2. 2. NS2 localizes mainly in the cytoplasm and promotes reduced nuclear accumulation of OsMYC2. 3. NS2 overexpression decreases OsBSMT1 transcripts, reduces parasitoid attraction, and increases vector preference, linking viral effector action to suppressed MeSA defense.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A viral protein rather than a plant metabolic enzyme or TF; functional analogs in other rice viruses are suggested but not identified here.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Attracts parasitoid wasps and repels planthopper vectors, contributing to indirect defense; potential field biocontrol signal
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Volatile emission from rice aerial tissues
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates salicylic acid to produce methyl salicylate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key enzyme/major rice SABATH for MeSA biosynthesis under SBPH infestation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strong promoter activity in seedling roots, stems, and leaves; transcripts induced by SBPH/WBPH/BPH/GRL infestation and reduced by several rice viruses including RSV, SRBSDV, RRSV, RBSDV, and rice grassy stunt virus
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased upon vector infestation; decreased in virus-infected plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes MeSA emission, parasitoid attraction, and vector repellence; virus-mediated down-regulation reduces indirect defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; Western blot; GUS reporter; GC-MS volatile quantification; olfactometer behavioral assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SBPH/WBPH/BPH/GRL infestation; infection by RSV, SRBSDV, RRSV, RBSDV, rice grassy stunt virus
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pUbi constitutive promoter; native OsBSMT1 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious phenotypic changes reported in overexpression or knockout lines under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering or breeding higher MeSA emission to enhance parasitoid-mediated biocontrol; may be combined with MeSA application or resistant cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsBSMT1 was the only detectable rice SABATH gene showing induction by SBPH and repression by RSV consistent with MeSA changes. 2. Overexpression increased MeSA emission and parasitoid attraction, whereas knockout abolished MeSA emission in SBPH-infested plants. 3. Multiple rice viruses suppress OsBSMT1 transcript accumulation, weakening indirect defense against vectors.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Defined by authors as the key enzyme catalyzing MeSA biosynthesis in rice; pUbi-driven OsBSMT1 was insensitive to RSV repression, indicating regulation occurs mainly at transcriptional promoter level.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Attracts parasitoids and repels insect vectors, strengthening indirect defense
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Volatile emission from rice aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; activity modulated by subcellular relocalization
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: MeSA biosynthesis via transcriptional control of OsBSMT1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsBSMT1 transcription by directly binding G-box motifs in the OsBSMT1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central JA signaling hub and key upstream activator of the OsBSMT1-MeSA pathway targeted by viruses
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonic acid signaling; insect vector infestation; viral infection
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms virus-induced cytoplasmic complex with RSV NS2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript levels increase after SBPH and other vector infestations; transcript can be reduced by some vector-borne rice viruses, while RSV mainly alters localization
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in OsMYC2ox, absent/reduced in osmyc2 mutant; nuclear abundance reduced by RSV infection
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus and cytoplasm; RSV shifts OsMYC2 from nucleus to cytoplasm
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes OsBSMT1 expression, MeSA-mediated parasitoid attraction, and vector deterrence; viral interference compromises these defenses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; protoplast transactivation; overexpression; mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; nuclear-cytoplasmic fractionation; immunofluorescence; olfactometer assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SBPH/WBPH/BPH/GRL infestation; RSV and other rice virus infection
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate regulatory node for improving volatile-mediated indirect defense, though manipulation may affect broader JA-responsive traits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYC2 directly binds the OsBSMT1 promoter and activates its transcription. 2. OsMYC2 overexpression elevates OsBSMT1-associated defense attractiveness, whereas osmyc2 reduces it. 3. RSV suppresses the pathway by redistributing OsMYC2 from nucleus to cytoplasm, impairing OsBSMT1 activation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors describe OsMYC2-OsBSMT1 as a central regulatory node integrating JA and SA signaling; OsMYC2 transcript was unchanged by RSV while total protein increased, indicating suppression occurs at activity/localization level rather than simple abundance change.
